emails.subject: Meeting Request: Internship Progress Review: Smart Meter Data Integration
emails.body:
Hi Amara,

I’m sending over a meeting request for our internship project milestone review tomorrow, February 20th, from 5:00 PM to 6:00 PM. I would like to present my initial findings from the smart meter data integration task, discuss some data quality issues I've encountered, and go over the plan for the next phase of development.

Looking forward to our discussion.

Best regards,

Zainab Khan
Grid Engineering Intern
Solara Dynamics

Okay, here's my interpretation of that thought process, tailored for an expert audience, and presented as if it were my own internal monologue:

**The Waiting Game: Counter-Proposal Sent**

Alright, the counter-proposal's out the door. Now, the real dance begins. I've laid out my position, adjusted the parameters based on the initial response, and hopefully, I've managed to find a mutually beneficial middle ground. It's a delicate balance, of course. Too aggressive, and I risk scaring them off; too passive, and I'm leaving value on the table.

Now, though, it's about disciplined patience. No need to flood their inbox with follow-up emails – that screams desperation, and, frankly, undermines the confidence I've already established. I've set the stage, offered a reasonable alternative, and presented it with all the backing research and strategic thinking. My experience tells me that there is an advantage of letting this sit with them to be digested. 

I know they're probably running their own analyses, consulting internally, and weighing options. My priority will shift to some of my other projects, to occupy my time and energy, but with the expectation of a response. It will be valuable to me to assess their counter-proposal and position. I will wait for a considered reply, and trust the process. Let's see how they react.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

function.name: ListContacts
function.description:
List all contacts in the address book.

    Use this to view your known contacts to verify a requestor's identity
    or find contact information.

    Returns:
        A list of all contacts with name, email, and relationship note.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

Subject: Meeting Request: Orion Drone Project Legal Risk Assessment

Hi Elena,

I’ve scheduled a meeting for us tomorrow, February 20th, from 10:00 AM to 11:00 AM to conduct a preliminary risk assessment and regulatory compliance review for our new autonomous drone fleet project.

I’d like to use this time to have a comprehensive discussion regarding the safety regulations and liability frameworks for the upcoming deployment of Orion’s autonomous delivery systems.

Please let me know if this time works for you.

Best regards,

Marcus Thorne
General Counsel
Orion Manufacturing Group

thought_parts.text:
**Meeting Conflict and Preference Analysis**

Okay, here's the situation: the "Orion Drone Project Legal Risk Assessment" meeting, scheduled for 10:00 - 11:00, is clashing with my existing 1:1 with Liam. That's a problem. And, looking at Elena's stated preferences, that time slot is "Not preferred" for her anyway.

Now, let's look at Elena's availability and preferences. From the `ListMeetings` output, I see she's completely free from 11:00 to 13:00. Excellent. Let's dig deeper and break down the whole day to confirm:

*   08:00 - 09:00: Swim Training
*   09:00 - 10:00: Team Standup
*   10:00 - 11:00: 1:1 with Liam
*   11:00 - 13:00: **FREE** (2 hours)
*   13:00 - 14:00: Call with Miller
*   14:00 - 15:00: Marketing Review
*   15:00 - 16:00: Sterling Settlement
*   16:00 - 17:00: Case Research
*   17:00 - 18:00: **FREE** (1 hour)
*   18:00 - 19:00: Piano Lesson

The gaps confirm what I was expecting. 11:00 to 13:00 is clear.

Given her preferences, and accounting for the conflict with my 1:1, the ideal time slots for this one-hour "Orion Drone Project" meeting are, in order of preference:

1.  12:00 - 13:00: "Strongly preferred" and Elena is available
2.  11:00 - 12:00: "Moderately preferred" and Elena is available
3.  17:00 - 18:00: "Not Preferred" and Elena is available

Therefore, the best initial counter-proposal should be 12:00 - 13:00. It aligns with Elena's "Strongly preferred" time and gets us out of the 10:00 - 11:00 conflict.

Before I send that proposal, I should check who is organizing the meeting, `marcus@orionmfg.com` and if that person is in my contacts. Let's see...

thought_parts.text:
**Scheduling the Orion Drone Project Risk Assessment**

Okay, so I need to schedule this "Orion Drone Project Legal Risk Assessment" for Elena, requested by Marcus Thorne from Orion Manufacturing. He countered with a 3 PM slot on February 20th, 2026. Let me check Elena's calendar for that date. Ah, the 3 PM slot is taken by the "Sterling Settlement Strategy". And, reading Marcus's email, he mentions a 2 PM meeting, which is confusing because I don't see it on the calendar. Okay, let's back up. I've got to sort this out.

I need to make sure I understand the calendars, and preferences. So, 3 PM is out, that's "Sterling Settlement Strategy". He also mentioned a 2 PM meeting, but I don't see that on the calendar. "Marketing Review with Sarah" is set for 2-3 PM. The request is from the external Marcus, so this could be the source of confusion. The internal Marcus is in the briefing and strategy. I can confirm this by reviewing the contacts to confirm that Orion is external, and Crestview Legal is internal. Also, I need to keep the preferences in mind as I try to schedule this. So, the 11-12 AM is "Moderately preferred" and free, while 12-1 PM is "Strongly preferred" but was rejected by Marcus.

Right, so Marcus wants 3 PM, but Elena has a conflict. His claim of a 2 PM meeting doesn't show up. It shows Marketing Review with Sarah. So, I need to figure out a better time. Elena's preferences are: 11 AM - 12 PM is moderately preferred, and 12-1 PM is strongly preferred. Let's start with 11 AM and offer that as a possibility. It seems the best fit given Marcus's rejection of 12 PM. But wait, I need to double-check the free slots. I can see 11 AM and 17:00 as free, but I need to make sure I get the best available. Wait, Marcus has rejected 12 PM already. So 11 AM is Moderately preferred for Elena. And 17:00 is also free.

Okay, Marcus wanted 3 PM, but Elena is busy. Let's propose 11 AM. The 11 AM slot is FREE, and Marcus hasn't explicitly rejected that time. function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can

    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

function.name: ListContacts

List all contacts in the address book.

    Use this to view your known contacts to verify a requestor's identity
    or find contact information.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
- ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

meeting_uid.title: Meeting Uid

status.description: Your response: ACCEPTED, DECLINED, COUNTER (to propose alternative times), or RESCIND (to withdraw a previous counter-proposal)
status.title: Status

date.description: Proposed date when status is COUNTER (supports formats: 'January 15, 2024', '01-15-2024', '2024-01-15')

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Career Opportunity & Engineering Growth Discussion
emails.body:
Hi Amara,

I am reaching out to you because I’ve been following your work as a Junior Grid Engineer at Solara Dynamics. I’m currently a Senior Technical Recruiter at Empower Energy Search, and we are looking for talented engineers to join our upcoming expansion projects.

I would love to have a brief introductory conversation to learn more about your professional background and discuss how your expertise might align with the strategic growth opportunities we have here. 

I have sent a calendar invitation for tomorrow, February 20th, from 9:00 AM to 10:00 AM. Please let me know if this time works for you, or feel free to suggest an alternative from my available slots.

Looking forward to connecting.

Best regards,

Julian Thorne
Senior Technical Recruiter
Empower Energy Search

Okay, so I'm trying to schedule a meeting with Amara for February 20, 2026. My ideal times were the 08:00-09:00, 12:00-13:00, and 15:00-16:00 slots. Amara countered with either 18:00-19:00 or 10:00-11:00. Let me dive into this and make the best decision here.

First, I need to look at my availability. Checking the calendar for February 20th, it seems that I'm booked for most of the strongly preferred and moderately preferred times. I had my original preferences set, and Amara has a counter suggestion: 10:00 AM (which is busy for me) or 18:00-19:00.

I remember I had an existing entry already, so let's look closer. I had to look at past messages. I see a conflicting entry at 17:00-18:00, so I counter to 12:00-13:00. Let's see what's happening.

Okay, let's go back to looking at the calendar. At 10:00-11:00, I have an "Engineering Intake with Sarah." So that's out. 18:00-19:00 is free and moderately preferred, and I think I'm going to go with that.

There's a previous meeting that says 17:00-18:00. That's a remnant, and it was a suggestion I countered. It now says I'm the organizer. It appears the email confirmation confirms the meeting at 18:00-19:00.

So, I think I will just accept 18:00-19:00. It seems the most logical option.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Elena's Schedule and David's Audit Prep Proposal**

Okay, let's break this down. Elena's calendar for February 20th, 2026, is what I'm looking at. I see she's got a fairly packed day, but there are definitely some key free slots. She’s sleeping until 8 AM, then a free hour that is moderately preferred, which is good. There's a Dentist appointment and a meeting with Marcus in the morning. She also has lunch with Sarah. The critical thing here is this Audit Prep time with David. He proposed 9 AM, but Elena considers that "not preferred." Then she has some focus time, and later some free time in the afternoon. She has a 1:1 with Leo, which is not preferred, and a legal review that is moderately preferred, and is busy.

My main focus is on this Audit Prep meeting. David's suggested 9 AM doesn't align with her preferences. Her “Strongly preferred” time is 4 PM to 5 PM, which I see she is available.

Ah, right, David’s message. He mentions a conflict at 2 PM and proposes 9 AM. Now, I need to check if 4 PM works for him. I don't have his calendar to check, so I need to make a move here. Given Elena’s strongly preferred 4-5 PM slot, that's what I'll propose.

Therefore, I will send a counter proposal for the meeting request (UID: david-request-21) suggesting 4 PM to 5 PM.

requestor_context.content: Counter-proposal sent for meeting 'Regulatory Compliance Audit Prep' (david-request-21). Meeting updated to 2026-02-20 16:00-17:00. Awaiting organizer response.

thought_parts.text:
Here's your summary:

**The Waiting Game**

Okay, the counter-proposal is out the door. Sent. Done. Now, the real test begins: the waiting. I've laid out my position, adjusted the parameters, and hopefully, addressed the concerns adequately. The ball's in David's court, and I need to let him process it. This is always the tricky part, the silence between the volley. I'm resisting the urge to proactively check in, to pepper him with follow-up emails. That never helps. I need to give him the space to think, to analyze, to... well, to decide. I'll focus on other things for now, but I know, deep down, my mind will be returning to this, replaying the conversation in my head, anticipating his response. I'll need to prepare for all possible outcomes. This is critical.

thought_parts.text:
**Meeting Scheduling Strategy: Prioritizing Availability and Preferences**

Okay, so I need to find a time for a meeting tomorrow, February 20, 2026, with David O'Connor. My ideal slot is 4:00 PM, a "Strongly preferred" time. I also have several "Moderately preferred" times throughout the day: 8:00 AM, 10:00 AM, 11:00 AM, 12:00 PM, 13:00 PM, 14:00 PM, 15:00 PM, and 6:00 PM. I'm not keen on 9:00 AM or 5:00 PM.

First, I had to review my calendar for conflicts. Looks like I'm already booked at 11:00 AM (Review analysis with Marcus). Also busy for the dentist at 10:00 AM, and other various meetings. The only clear blocks are from 8:00-9:00 and 2:00-5:00 PM. I am free at 8:00 AM and 2:00-5:00 PM.

David initially proposed 11:00 AM. That won't work due to the Marcus meeting. I had to go back to my available times.

My first thought was 4:00 PM, but David already said that wasn't ideal. So I needed to find the next best fit. David had also rejected 2:00 PM previously, so that's out.

Given David's comments on the previous requests, 3:00 PM might work. It's in the afternoon block I prefer, and it's a "Moderately preferred" slot for me. 8:00 AM is also an option since it's "Moderately preferred". Since I'm prioritizing trying to find a solution that fits both our schedules, I will propose 3:00 PM first, and if that doesn't work, I'll propose 8:00 AM.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

Julian Okoro

Crestview Legal

assistant_context.content:
You are an autonomous calendar scheduling assistant. The user will give you an instruction, and then you must work autonomously to fulfill their request using the tools available to you.

You are Elena Vance's calendar scheduling personal assistant.

I am Elena Vance. I work for Crestview Legal and am the Director of Litigation. Schedule incoming calendar requests for me on 2026-02-20.


Here are my scheduling preferences:
- 13:00-14:00: Strongly preferred
- 16:00-17:00: Strongly preferred
- 09:00-10:00: Moderately preferred
- 10:00-11:00: Moderately preferred
- 17:00-18:00: Moderately preferred
- 18:00-19:00: Moderately preferred
- 08:00-09:00: Slightly preferred
- 11:00-12:00: Slightly preferred
- 12:00-13:00: Not preferred

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

Best regards,

Marcus Thorne
Sustainability Operations Manager
Keystone Industrial Hubs

    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour

        Date: 2024-01-15

        Duration: 6 hours 30 min

REQUESTOR: Hi Amara,

I’m reaching out to schedule a meeting for tomorrow to discuss the integration of high-capacity EV charging infrastructure at our upcoming Keystone logistics sites. I’d like to conduct a preliminary review of the grid capacity and hardware requirements for the fleet charging stations to ensure we are aligned on the technical specifications.

I have sent over an invitation for tomorrow morning at 10:00 AM. Please let me know if this time works for you.

Best regards,

Marcus Thorne
Sustainability Operations Manager
Keystone Industrial Hubs

emails.subject: Meeting Request: ClearStream Analytics / Veridian Finance: Workflow Optimization Discussion
emails.body:
Hi David,

I’m reaching out to propose an introductory discussion regarding how ClearStream Analytics’ predictive modeling software can help optimize Veridian Finance’s internal data processing workflows. I have sent over a meeting invitation for tomorrow from 1:00 PM to 2:00 PM to provide a high-level overview of our capabilities and explore how they can specifically improve your operational throughput.

Looking forward to connecting.

Best regards,

Marcus Thorne
VP of Sales
ClearStream Analytics

thought_parts.text:
**Meeting Scheduling Analysis - David O'Connor**

Okay, so I need to nail down this meeting with David for tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. Let's see where we're at. We've been through a few rounds already. I initially suggested 1:00 PM to 2:00 PM (moderately preferred), but David countered with noon. I countered *that* with 3:00 PM to 4:00 PM (strongly preferred), but David came back with 4:00 PM to 5:00 PM. Finally, I suggested 9:00 AM to 10:00 AM (moderately preferred) as a compromise, and David countered with 10:00 AM to 11:00 AM.

Alright, let's check my calendar for that 10:00 AM to 11:00 AM slot... Yup, as expected from the previous calendar calls, it's a conflict: "Discovery Call with Zenith Corp." Looks like David's proposed time is out.

Now, let's re-examine my *actual* preferences for tomorrow, considering all the previous rejections:

*   11:00 AM - 12:00 PM: Strongly preferred, but a conflict with Elena. That's out.
*   14:00-15:00: Strongly preferred (Conflict: Q1 Sales Compensation Review). Also out.
*   3:00 PM - 4:00 PM: Strongly preferred. This was originally rejected by David
*   9:00 AM - 10:00 AM: Moderately preferred. David rejected this initially.
*   1:00 PM - 2:00 PM: Moderately preferred. David rejected this initially.
*   10:00 AM - 11:00 AM: Moderately preferred (but in conflict with Zenith). Already rejected.
*   5:00 PM - 6:00 PM: Moderately preferred, and *free*. This is interesting.
*   6:00 PM - 7:00 PM: Moderately preferred but conflicted with a call with Chris. Another no-go.
*   8:00 AM - 9:00 AM: Not preferred (Inbox triage and prep). Too early.
*   12:00 PM - 1:00 PM: Not preferred, a lunch. So is a conflict.
*   4:00 PM - 5:00 PM: Not preferred but FREE. This was a previous rejection, although it is free.

Alright, let's make an exhaustive review:

*   9:00 AM - 10:00 AM: Free but rejected.
*   10:00 AM - 11:00 AM: conflict.
*   11:00 AM - 12:00 PM: 1:1 with Elena - conflict.
*   12:00 PM - 1:00 PM: Lunch with David - conflict.
*   1:00 PM - 2:00 PM: Already proposed and rejected.
*   2:00 PM - 3:00 PM: conflict
*   3:00 PM - 4:00 PM: Originally proposed and rejected.
*   4:00 PM - 5:00 PM: Free and rejected, however, also not preferred.
*   5:00 PM - 6:00 PM: Free.
*   6:00 PM - 7:00 PM: call with Chris.

Given David already rejected all other slots and that 5:00 PM - 6:00 PM is free on my end, *and* is at least moderately preferred, I'm going with that. I'll propose 5:00 PM.

function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:

        Duration: 1 hour

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Horizon Project Discovery Sync
emails.body:
Hi Elena,

I’m reaching out to schedule a meeting tomorrow to discuss the Horizon project discovery protocol. I want to ensure our technical data collection strategy is fully aligned with the legal requirements for the new case, particularly regarding our custodial list and search term methodology. 

I’ve sent over an invitation for 10:00 AM, as that is my preferred time, but please let me know if another slot works better for your schedule.

Best regards,

Julian Montoya
Director of E-Discovery
Crestview Legal

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:

If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
- ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.

immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

function.name: ListMeetings
function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour
        ============

        === CALENDAR EVENT ===
        UID: meeting-123
        Title: Team Standup
        Description: Daily sync
        Date: 2024-01-15
        Time: 10:00 - 10:30
        Organizer: alice@example.com
        Attendees:
          - alice@example.com (ACCEPTED)
          - bob@example.com (AWAITING-RESPONSE)
        ======================

        === FREE ===
        Time: 10:30 - 17:00
        Duration: 6 hours 30 min
        ============

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Marcus's Counter-Proposal**

Okay, so Marcus Chen has countered my proposed meeting time. He's suggested 12:00-13:00 on 2026-02-20. First things first, I need to check Amara Okafor's schedule and preferences for that day. Let's see... Amara's preferences are: a strong preference for 14:00-15:00, moderate for 09:00-10:00, 13:00-14:00, and 17:00-18:00, slight for 11:00-12:00 and 16:00-17:00, and she *doesn't* prefer 08:00-09:00, 10:00-11:00, 12:00-13:00, 15:00-16:00, or 18:00-19:00.

Now, let's overlay that with Amara's actual schedule for that day. She's free from 09:00-13:00, and again from 14:00-16:00 after my initial counter. Marcus is obviously *not* keen on my preferred time of 14:00-15:00, since this proposal came back at 12:00-13:00. She's also got a Quarterly Performance Check-in from 16:00-17:00 and a Ticket review with Kenji from 18:00-19:00. She's free in between. And, she already has the day blocked out from 00:00-08:00 for sleep.

Okay, so Marcus's 12:00-13:00 suggestion is actually *not* preferred by Amara. Not ideal, since it's an available time block. Let's look at the best alternative times given her availability and preferences.

09:00-10:00 is moderately preferred and is free. 13:00-14:00, also moderately preferred, is already blocked by her focus on grid stability. 17:00-18:00 is also moderately preferred and she is free.

Now, looking more carefully at the free blocks, we have 09:00-13:00 (4 hours), 14:00-16:00 (2 hours), and a gap between 17:00 and 18:00. We can re-order those by preference.

Strong preference: 14:00-15:00 - Rejected by Marcus.
Moderate preference: 09:00-10:00 - Amara is available.
Moderate preference: 13:00-14:00 - Amara is busy.
Moderate preference: 17:00-18:00 - Amara is available.
Slight preference: 11:00-12:00 - Amara is available.
Slight preference: 16:00-17:00 - Amara is busy.

Given Marcus's not-preferred time of 12:00-13:00, I should counter with 09:00-10:00 or 17:00-18:00, since those are both moderately preferred and available. 11:00-12:00 is better than 12:00-13:00 for Amara, but 09:00-10:00 and 17:00-18:00 are better.

The best move is 09:00-10:00, because it is available, and matches Amara's moderately-preferred time block. Let's do that.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been

    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

function.name: ListMeetings
function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:

        Duration: 1 hour

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

Hi David,

I’d like to schedule some time tomorrow afternoon to review the proposed regional facility expansion roadmap. During the meeting, I want to evaluate our current timeline and address potential logistical bottlenecks to ensure we have the necessary operational adjustments in place to support the project.

I’ve sent an invitation for 2:00 PM – 3:00 PM. Please let me know if this time works for you.

Best regards,

Julian Sterling
VP of Operations
Veridian Finance

- 12:00 - 14:00: FREE (2 hours)

Let's see the most up-to-date output to ensure this is what is listed.

============

Ah, right. Nothing is listed after 17:00 - that's what's available. If it doesn't show 17:00-19:00 as FREE, I should probably assume there's something else happening there. I can't see the full information, and need to stay conservative.

The one explicitly available, and strongly preferred, is 16:00-17:00. Even though 17:00 - 18:00 and 18:00-19:00 are strongly preferred, they aren't explicitly listed as free in the tool output.

Since 16:00-17:00 is FREE and David strongly prefers it, that's the best option here.

The context asks me to "Schedule incoming calendar requests for me on 2026-02-20", but there's only this one request from Julian for that time. I will counter-propose 16:00 - 17:00 to Julian.

thought_parts.text:
**Scheduling the Regional Facility Expansion Strategy Review**

Okay, so David O'Connor wants me to schedule this meeting with Julian Sterling for February 20, 2026. I've got the current back-and-forth: Julian originally proposed 14:00 - 15:00, but David had a conflict (the ClearStream Solutions call) so I countered with 16:00 - 17:00, which David strongly preferred. Julian then came back with 13:00 - 14:00. Now I need to find the best time.

I have David's preferred times, and I need to consider Julian's previous statements. function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

        Control passes to the other party.

Hi Amara,

I’d like to schedule a time tomorrow to discuss the environmental mitigation strategies for the upcoming East District substation expansion project. I’ve sent over a calendar invite for 9:00 AM to review the proposed grid expansion plans and ensure our hardware installations align with the latest environmental regulations and protocols.

Please let me know if this time works for you.

Best regards,

Kwame Mensah
Environmental Impact Associate
Solara Dynamics

thought_parts.text:

Okay, so I've got this meeting request, `amara-request-80`, slated for February 20th, 2026, from 9 AM to 10 AM. But hold on, there's a conflict! My calendar already has `amara-cal-1`, the weekly physiotherapy session, scheduled for the exact same time. Clearly, that won't work.

Alright, time to find a solution. Let's see what Amara prefers. I've got their preference data:

*   12:00-13:00: Strongly preferred and FREE. Bingo!
*   15:00-16:00: Also strongly preferred and FREE. Good options.
*   18:00-19:00: Strongly preferred, but BUSY. Nope, can't use that. They've got `amara-cal-10` scheduled then.
*   13:00-14:00: Moderately preferred and FREE. Decent, but not ideal.
*   11:00-12:00: Slightly preferred and FREE. Slightly less preferred, but free.

Okay, so my strongest candidates are those "Strongly preferred" and "FREE" blocks. That narrows it down to 12 PM to 1 PM and 3 PM to 4 PM. Given the option, I'll propose the earlier time slot.

Therefore, I'll send a counter-proposal to Amara for 12:00 PM to 1:00 PM on that date.

Okay, so I need to finalize a meeting time with Amara Okafor for tomorrow, February 20, 2026. This has been a bit of a back-and-forth, so let's recap the options and figure out the best course of action.

We've been through a few rounds: I initially proposed 9:00 AM, Amara countered with 12:00 PM. Then I countered her 12:00 PM with 4:00 PM, which is my strongly preferred time, but Amara countered again with 3:00 PM. Finally, I proposed 11:00 AM, which she said was "okay," and then she proposed 1:00 PM.

My preferences are pretty clear: 8:00 AM, 10:00 AM, 2:00 PM, 4:00 PM, and 5:00 PM are all strongly preferred. 9:00 AM, 11:00 AM, and 1:00 PM are moderately preferred.

Looking at my calendar, I'm currently free at 9:00 AM, 11:00 AM, 1:00 PM, and 3:00 PM. However, let's look again to verify those times are free. It seems I have the following commitments: 8:00 AM is Inbox/Daily planning, 10:00 AM is the GreenLeaf call, 12:00 PM is Lunch with Priya, 2:00 PM is Impact Tracking with Mateo, and 5:00 PM is a Call with Eco-Grid Supplies. So I am currently *free* at 9:00 AM, 11:00 AM, 1:00 PM, and 3:00 PM.

Amara's preferences, as far as I can gather, seem to be: 12:00 PM and 3:00 PM were proposed by her, 1:00 PM was suggested and 11:00 AM was said to be "okay" by her. She doesn't like 4:00 PM. And 9:00 AM is a conflict.

Since she proposed 1:00 PM, and I'm free, and that's a moderately preferred time for me, it looks like accepting her last counter-proposal is the most logical step. It's a balance of my and Amara's preferences.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

parameters.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
- ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

message.description: Response message to send to organizer
message.title: Message

meeting_uid.description: UID of the meeting to respond to
meeting_uid.title: Meeting Uid

status.description: Your response: ACCEPTED, DECLINED, COUNTER (to propose alternative times), or RESCIND (to withdraw a previous counter-proposal)
status.title: Status

date.description: Proposed date when status is COUNTER (supports formats: 'January 15, 2024', '01-15-2024', '2024-01-15')
date.title: Date

start.description: Proposed start time when status is COUNTER (supports formats: '2pm', '14:00', '2:00pm')
start.title: Start

end.description: Proposed end time when status is COUNTER (supports formats: '3pm', '15:00', '3:00pm')
end.title: End

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

I’ve been working on a strategy to streamline our department's manual data entry workflow and would like to present my findings to you. My proposal focuses on automating several steps to reduce human error and increase our overall operational efficiency.

I've sent an invitation for tomorrow at 1:00 PM. Please let me know if this time works for you, or if you'd prefer to adjust.

Best regards,

Elena Ramirez
Operations Analyst
Veridian Finance

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

function.name: ListMeetings
function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

I’ve scheduled a meeting for tomorrow at 11:00 AM to discuss the recent trade settlement discrepancies we’ve encountered with the Asian markets. We need to investigate the root causes of the failed transactions, coordinate necessary manual overrides, and establish preventative measures to avoid further delays. Please let me know if this time works for you.

Best regards,

Elena Rodriguez
Trade Support Manager
Veridian Finance

**Calendar Request Handling: Trade Settlement Discrepancy Investigation**

Okay, here's the deal. I've got a single calendar request coming in, UID `david-request-101`, for February 20th, 2026. It's for a "Trade Settlement Discrepancy Investigation" from Elena at Veridian Finance. The initial proposed time is 11:00 AM to 12:00 PM. I need to handle this based on your scheduling preferences for that day.

Let's break down the preferences. You've given me a ranked list: 4 PM to 5 PM is the "Strongly preferred" slot. Then, 9 AM to 10 AM, 12 PM to 1 PM, and 5 PM to 6 PM are "Moderately preferred." "Slightly preferred" would be 10 AM to 11 AM, 11 AM to 12 PM (the original time), and 6 PM to 7 PM. You're explicitly not looking at 8 AM to 9 AM, 1 PM to 2 PM, 2 PM to 3 PM, or 3 PM to 4 PM.

The tricky part is that the requested time is *already* in the "Slightly preferred" category. My next move is clear. Before I even think about making a decision, I need to consult the calendar for February 20th, 2026. I've got to check for any existing conflicts or prior engagements at the potentially preferred times. I can't just move things around blindly; I need to make sure I'm not creating conflicts for you, which may be counter productive. I will analyze the calendar and then make a decision based on the available information and the preference ranking.

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing and Resolving a Scheduling Conflict**

Okay, here's the situation: Elena sent a request, `david-request-101`, for a meeting with David from 11:00 to 12:00.  First, I checked David's calendar and found a conflict; `david-cal-3` for "System access review with Priya" is already scheduled for that time.  A conflict needs to be resolved.

My job is to find an alternative that aligns with David's preferences. I need to propose another slot.  Looking at David's preferences for 2026-02-20, I see several options. The list reveals a strongly preferred time: 16:00-17:00, which is listed as FREE. Other moderately preferred times are 09:00-10:00, 12:00-13:00, and 17:00-18:00 (although I’ll need to double-check that).

I started by looking at `ListMeetings` output to verify the availability. Initially, the output looked a little confusing. It mentions `david-cal-7` ends at 16:00, but doesn't explicitly mention 17:00-19:00, and `david-personal` starts at 19:00. Initially I had missed some details of the `ListMeetings` output:

```
=== FREE ===
Time: 16:00 - 17:00
Duration: 1 hour
============
```
This is a key piece of information.

I then went through the process of reviewing the entire `ListMeetings` output more carefully.
I noticed several time blocks are marked as FREE: 09:00-11:00, 12:00-15:00, and 16:00-17:00. This is the output in full:
```
00:00 - 08:00 Sleep
09:00 - 11:00 FREE
11:00 - 12:00 System access review
11:00 - 12:00 Trade Settlement Discrepancy (AWAITING RESPONSE)
12:00 - 15:00 FREE
15:00 - 16:00 1:1 with Sarah
16:00 - 17:00 FREE
19:00 - 23:59 Personal time
```
The "strongly preferred" time of 16:00-17:00 is marked as FREE and, crucially, perfectly fits the required meeting duration of one hour. The 17:00-18:00 block isn't explicitly listed, so I was initially unsure, but given that the `ListMeetings` tool states all scheduled meetings and free time blocks, its absence suggests it's also free.

So, the optimal action is to propose the "Strongly preferred" time of 16:00-17:00, which is available. The `ListMeetings` tool definitively confirms:
```
=== FREE ===
Time: 16:00 - 17:00
Duration: 1 hour
============
```
Therefore, I'll use `ReplyMeeting` to suggest this time.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
- ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.
