function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:

If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
- ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

So, 13:00-14:00 is off the table as far as I'm concerned. Let's see what else might work that is acceptable to me. Since she's gone against my original Strongly preferred slot, I should counter with another slot from my list of preferences. function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: QuantBase SLA and Support Workflow Finalization
emails.body:
Hi David,

I’m reaching out to finalize our Service Level Agreements and support workflows for our ongoing partnership. In this meeting, I’d like to align on the escalation matrix, response times, and maintenance windows for the new infrastructure to ensure a smooth post-launch support phase.

I have sent over an invitation for tomorrow from 6:00 PM to 7:00 PM. Please let me know if this time works for you, or if you'd prefer to adjust.

Best regards,

Marcus Chen
Senior Solutions Architect
QuantBase Systems

**Calendar Scheduling for QuantBase SLA Meeting**

Okay, so I've got a new calendar request to handle. The user wants me to schedule a meeting for February 20th, 2026. The request comes from Marcus Chen at QuantBase Systems – `marcus@quantbase-systems.com` – and it's for a meeting called "QuantBase SLA and Support Workflow Finalization," which needs to be held on that date, February 20th, from 6:00 PM to 7:00 PM.

My first step is to check David's calendar for that day. I need to know his availability before I do anything else. Once I have that information, I'll need to confirm that this requested time slot, the 6:00 PM to 7:00 PM window, actually works. They have a time preference, so I need to check the preference for that time slot. It looks like 6:00-7:00 PM is "Slightly preferred," so it should be fine.

Based on David's availability and considering the preference for the time slot, I'll make a decision. I can either accept the request as is, or I may need to counter-propose a different time if there are any conflicts. I'll get started and look up the 2026-02-20 meetings.

thought_parts.text:
**Meeting Scheduling Decision: Prioritizing QuantBase Finalization**

Okay, so I've got this meeting request, `david-request-121`, for the "QuantBase SLA and Support Workflow Finalization," scheduled for 18:00 to 19:00 on February 20, 2026. *But*, there's a conflict: `david-cal-10`, the Astronomy Club logistics meeting, is scheduled for the *exact* same time.

First things first: I need to find a suitable alternative. Let's look at available time. Okay, `ListMeetings` shows my schedule: sleep from 00:00 to 08:00, nothing listed from 08:00 to 09:00, astronomy at 18:00 to 19:00, and personal time after that. There's a big block from 09:00 to 17:00 that's marked as *FREE*. And the request is from David.

Now, let's consider the preferences. The preferences are:
*   10:00-11:00: **Strongly preferred**
*   12:00-13:00: **Strongly preferred**
*   14:00-15:00: **Strongly preferred**
*   16:00-17:00: Moderately preferred
*   08:00-09:00: Slightly preferred

It's clear that the first strongly preferred slot, 10:00-11:00, is the best. It aligns with the available free time, and it satisfies the preferences. The meeting request is one hour, so 10:00-11:00 should work perfectly.

Before sending the reply, I should double check if there are other incoming requests, but I already checked the emails and the `ListMeetings` output, so it looks like this is the only one.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

Hi Elena,

I’m reaching out to schedule our Quarterly Talent Review for the Litigation Department. I’d like to sync up tomorrow to review the upcoming performance evaluation timelines and discuss staffing requirements for your open positions. 

I've proposed 10:00–11:00 AM as it's a preferred time on my end, but please let me know if a different slot works better for you. 

Looking forward to our discussion.

Best,

Aisha Okafor
Director of Human Resources
Crestview Legal

thought_parts.text:
**Calendar Scheduling Considerations for Elena Vance**

Okay, here's the situation. Elena Vance has tasked me with handling her calendar requests for February 20, 2026. My primary task is to manage requests for that day. Currently, there's a meeting request from Aisha Okafor, regarding the "Quarterly Talent Review for Litigation Department" scheduled for 10:00 to 11:00 AM on that date. That's UID elena-request-122. I need to keep that UID handy.

The thing is, Elena's preferences explicitly indicate that the 10:00-11:00 AM time slot on the 20th is *not* ideal. Given this, my immediate next step is clear: I need to consult Elena's calendar for that specific day to identify a more suitable time for this Quarterly Talent Review meeting. I need to proactively find available time slots that align better with her stated preferences to schedule that meeting correctly.

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Elena's Availability for the Quarterly Talent Review**

Okay, so I need to reschedule this "Quarterly Talent Review for Litigation Department" for Elena. The original time, 10:00-11:00, is a no-go – it's "Not Preferred" and conflicts with Elena's 1:1 with Liam.

Let's dive into Elena's 2026-02-20 availability. The system is showing some inconsistencies, so I need to be meticulous.  It's flagged that 08:00-09:00 is "Strongly preferred," but the initial "FREE" blocks list seems a bit… wonky. Sleep runs until 08:00, then the "FREE" list jumps to 09:00-10:00, skipping that critical strongly preferred time. I need to make sure I understand the gaps.

Let me go over this step by step. I will extract the blocks shown in the `ListMeetings` output.
- Sleep: 00:00 - 08:00
- Free: 09:00 - 10:00
- Liam: 10:00 - 11:00
- Free: 11:00 - 17:00
- Personal: 19:00 - 23:59

What about 08:00-09:00, 17:00-18:00, and 18:00-19:00? They aren't listed as FREE, but they aren't CALENDAR EVENTS either. Why are they missing? I need to check for some other potential source of these "missing blocks".

Ok, these missing blocks aren't on the list! Elena's preferences are 08:00-09:00 (Strongly Preferred) and 14:00-17:00 (Strongly Preferred), 18:00-19:00 (Strongly Preferred), with 09:00-10:00 (Moderately preferred).  The availability list aligns with those preferences, in blocks.

The "FREE" blocks given in the ListMeetings output are:
- 09:00 - 10:00 (Moderately preferred).
- 11:00 - 17:00 (Strongly preferred).

Since the original time is out, I'm going to focus on those "Strongly Preferred" and "Free" blocks within the day. So, 14:00-15:00, 15:00-16:00, and 16:00-17:00.

I've got the 1-hour meeting requirement, and the 14:00-15:00 slot is the first available "Strongly Preferred" slot. That looks like the best option.

Alright, time to propose a counter offer. I'll send back a COUNTER response for that elena-request-122. I'm going to offer a reschedule.

**Counter Response Details:**

*   **UID:** elena-request-122
*   **Organizer:** aisha@crestviewlegal.com (Director of HR)
*   **Status:** COUNTER
*   **Start:** 14:00
*   **End:** 15:00

Okay, let's nail down this talent review session with Elena Vance for tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. I've been back and forth with her a bit already, proposing times. Now, things get a little tricky with how this tool represents status. The "event" field in the last result initially showed *my* status as "ACCEPTED" and Elena's as "AWAITING-RESPONSE." That's a little confusing because she sent that "ACCEPTED" email! However, the tool *does* update via the `ReplyMeeting` output which is great.

Let's break this down further from the interactions. I sent the counter-proposal for 9-10. Then, Elena sent that email with the "Meeting ACCEPTED" subject. The email details in the result, `elena-request-122`, show the 9:00 AM - 10:00 AM time slot. The system is interpreting my status as "ACCEPTED" because I was the one who sent the counter-proposal.

But, hang on... looking again at the email data, the `event` block *begins* with "ACCEPTED:". That's significant. The structure implies that *she* is signaling her acceptance. But the `Attendees:` section is where it gets slightly inconsistent with the message subject... it's showing `Elena` as still AWAITING-RESPONSE. However, the subject is the real signal here: "Meeting ACCEPTED" in her reply to my counter-proposal.

Considering Elena's explicit "Meeting ACCEPTED" subject and the fact that it directly followed my counter-proposal for 9-10, this is pretty clear. So, if her email says "ACCEPTED", then we are good to go. She accepted the 9-10 slot.

To be absolutely certain, I'll quickly double-check the calendar to verify the meeting is confirmed and scheduled for 9:00-10:00. No need to leave any room for doubt.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.
